scope: Compressed Air Leak Cost Calculator supports one operation: compressed_air_leak_cost_assessment. It is limited to decision-support screening using user-supplied, source-verified inputs for estimating leakage flow, energy loss, annual cost, and repair payback.

decision_context.system_boundary: Compressed air system leak assessment — single leak point analysis. Includes orifice geometry, system pressure, operating schedule, compressor efficiency, electricity cost, and estimated repair cost.
decision_context.single_operation_scope: Evaluate compressed air leak cost as a single decision-support operation using normalized inputs, choked flow gas dynamics, evidence checks, and business impact.
decision_context.decision_owner: Engineer, maintenance technician, site lead, operations manager, energy manager
decision_context.decision_after_output: schedule repair, prioritize leak, budget allocation, adjust maintenance schedule, or escalate to energy audit
decision_context.cost_of_wrong_decision: Underestimated leak cost delays repair, causing continuous energy waste. Overestimated cost misallocates maintenance resources.
decision_context.failure_mode_if_formula_wrong: Wrong unit normalization, incorrect choked flow assumption, or missing evidence can produce misleading cost estimates and wrong repair priority.
required_source_documents: calibrated measurement record for orifice diameter
compressor specification or measurement for specific power
electricity tariff or utility bill for cost per kWh
maintenance estimate or quote for repair cost
excluded_use_cases: system-wide compressed air audit without multi-point measurement
field_use_limitations: Use for screening and decision support only.
Assumes choked flow condition at orifice (critical pressure ratio met).
Single leak point analysis only — do not sum multiple leaks without independent verification.
Verify all inputs from approved source documents or direct measurement.

irreversible_commitment_metric: maintenance work order, repair expense, or capital allocation based on payback estimate

standards.name: ISO 8573-1 compressed air quality standard context
ISO 11011 compressed air energy efficiency assessment context
Ideal gas law and choked flow gas dynamics (compressible flow theory)
standards.role: Choked mass flow rate calculation through orifice

brand_safety_policy.allowed_description: Compressed air leak cost engineering assessment

calculation_basis.calculation_character: Protected multi-node formula graph for Compressed Air Leak Cost Calculator: orifice geometry, choked flow gas dynamics, energy loss, cost, payback, and decision state.

unit_system.name: SI_BASE_UNITS_WITH_DOMAIN_EXCEPTIONS
unit_system.display_unit_policy: User-selectable display units with base-unit normalization before server execution.

physical_hard_bounds.semantic_error_message_min: Leak orifice diameter must be at least 0.1 mm.
physical_hard_bounds.semantic_error_message_max: Leak orifice diameter exceeds maximum (20 mm).

engineering_range.source: Typical compressed air leak orifice sizes.

reference_values.source: Orifice diameter must be measured or estimated from leak type.
reference_values.public_note: Enter the estimated or measured leak orifice diameter.

evidence_requirement.public_help_text: Confirm leak orifice diameter source evidence.

physical_hard_bounds.semantic_error_message_min: System pressure must be at least 1 bar(g).

engineering_range.source: Typical industrial compressed air system pressure range.

reference_values.source: System pressure must be measured at the leak location.
reference_values.public_note: Enter the compressed air system pressure.

physical_hard_bounds.semantic_error_message_min: Operating hours must be at least 1 hour/year.

reference_values.source: Operating hours must match the facility production schedule.
reference_values.public_note: Enter annual operating hours for the compressed air system.

physical_hard_bounds.semantic_error_message_min: Specific power must be at least 3 kW per m3/min.
physical_hard_bounds.semantic_error_message_max: Specific power exceeds maximum (12 kW per m3/min).

engineering_range.source: Typical compressor specific power range (lubricated rotary screw).

reference_values.source: Specific power from compressor manufacturer data or measurement.
reference_values.public_note: Enter the compressor specific power consumption.

evidence_requirement.public_help_text: Confirm compressor specific power source evidence.

reference_values.source: Electricity cost from utility bill or tariff schedule.
reference_values.public_note: Enter the blended or marginal electricity cost.

engineering_range.source: Typical compressed air leak repair cost range.

reference_values.source: Repair cost estimate from maintenance quote or historical data.
reference_values.public_note: Enter the estimated cost to repair the leak.

outputs.name: Estimated Air Loss
outputs.public_explanation: Volumetric flow rate of compressed air lost through the leak orifice under choked flow conditions.
outputs.operator_explanation: Air volume escaping through the leak per minute.
outputs.checker_explanation: Verify orifice diameter and system pressure inputs.

outputs.name: Annual Energy Loss
outputs.public_explanation: Annual energy consumed to produce the compressed air lost through the leak.
outputs.operator_explanation: kWh lost through the leak per year.
outputs.checker_explanation: Verify operating hours and specific power inputs.

outputs.name: Annual Leak Cost
outputs.public_explanation: Total annual cost of electricity wasted through the compressed air leak.
outputs.owner_cfo_explanation: Direct financial impact of the leak.

outputs.name: Repair Payback Period
outputs.public_explanation: Days of energy savings needed to recover the repair cost.
outputs.operator_explanation: Days until repair pays for itself.

outputs.name: Decision Status
outputs.public_explanation: Action priority based on payback period and annual leak cost.
outputs.operator_explanation: What to do about this leak.
outputs.checker_explanation: Verify against payback and cost thresholds.

outputs.name: Governing Driver
outputs.public_explanation: Primary factor driving the decision status classification.
outputs.operator_explanation: Why this leak has this priority.

formulas.name: Compressed air loss model via choked flow equation
assumptions: Choked flow condition holds (critical pressure ratio is met).
Air is an ideal gas with constant specific heat ratio of 1.4.
Discharge coefficient of 0.65 accounts for vena contracta and friction losses.
Ambient temperature is 20°C (293.15 K) for density calculation.
Single leak point is independent of other leaks in the system.
limitations: This is a decision-support screening model and is not a certified energy audit.
Does not account for compressor part-load efficiency degradation.
Does not account for distribution piping pressure losses.
Single leak point analysis only; multiple leaks must be calculated separately.
edge_cases: Zero electricity cost produces zero annual leak cost (payback undefined).
Pressure below atmospheric reverses flow direction (not supported).
formulas.public_proof_summary: Computes compressed air leakage rate and associated energy cost using choked flow gas dynamics.
formulas.protected_methodology_summary: Server-side formula registry applies choked flow equation with discharge coefficient, absolute pressure, orifice area, and compressor specific power.
formulas.checker_note: Verify orifice diameter is the equivalent diameter of the leak opening, not the fitting size.
formulas.acceptance_logic: Leak cost is acceptable when annual leak cost is below advisory threshold.
formulas.rejection_logic: Block or reject when inputs fail physical bounds or non-finite check.
formulas.review_required_logic: Review when governing driver indicates moderate or high urgency.
formulas.uncertainty_expression: Combined standard uncertainty is computed server-side from input measurement uncertainty.

rules.why_it_matters: Fail-closed validation prevents false confidence and invalid results.
rules.suggested_action: Provide all required inputs before submitting.

rules.condition_description: input value outside physical hard bounds
rules.semantic_message: Input value is outside physically possible range.
rules.why_it_matters: Physically impossible values produce invalid results.
rules.suggested_action: Correct the input value to a physically possible range.

rules.condition_description: Electricity cost is zero, annual leak cost will be zero.
rules.semantic_message: Zero electricity cost produces zero annual leak cost.
rules.why_it_matters: Leak cost and payback cannot be calculated with zero energy cost.

ui_contract.tool_subtitle: Estimate leakage flow, annual energy loss, leak cost, and repair payback

metadata.change_log_summary: V5.4 Core — First verified Pro pilot. Compressed Air Leak Cost Calculator using choked flow gas dynamics.
metadata.author: SectorCalc V5.4 Core

public_report_rules: Report is for decision-support only and is not a certified document.
Verify all inputs from approved source documents.
Choked flow assumption applies only when critical pressure ratio is met.

fields.primary_reason: Server-owned reason based on margin, evidence, uncertainty, FMEA, and money-at-risk.

user_profile_summary.operator: Use the status and blockers to decide whether the operation can proceed.
user_profile_summary.engineer: Review margin, evidence, uncertainty, derating, and governing driver before approval.
user_profile_summary.owner_cfo: Use money-at-risk to decide whether to reprice, hold, repair, outsource, or reject.
user_profile_summary.checker_auditor: Verify audit seal, normalized input audit, redaction status, and proof-pack completeness.

hidden_risk_explanations: Narrow margin can flip under measurement uncertainty.
Missing evidence can invalidate an apparently acceptable result.
Commercial exposure may exceed technical issue size.
next_best_actions: Export proof pack only after public-safe redaction succeeds.
fields.premium_unlock_reason: Unlock protected methodology, source-evidence proof pack, uncertainty, FMEA, sensitivity, and commercial impact in one auditable decision record.

business_impact_contract.public_value_statement: Connects the technical leak assessment to annual cost and payback without hidden FX conversion or accounting-proof claims.
business_impact_contract.premium_unlock_reason: Shows whether the next best business action is schedule repair, prioritize, or monitor.

state_messages.PASS: Leak cost is within acceptable range.
state_messages.WARNING: Leak cost is significant; verify inputs.
state_messages.CRITICAL: Leak cost is severe; schedule repair immediately.

state_messages.WARNING: Payback period is long; verify repair cost.

audit_trail_contract.tamper_warning: Changing any input, unit, schema version, formula version, or output invalidates the audit seal.

test_plan: schema generation prompt not used in runtime
normalized preview does not execute formulas
reference values are visible but do not autofill critical inputs
FMEA trigger at high utilization or threshold risk

canonical RedactionStatus consistency between ExecuteResponse and AuditSeal
state machine is actually bound to UniversalIndustrialDecisionForm
approved schema fetch path exists and missing schema blocks execution
private formula registry is server-only and not imported by client code
proof-pack-builder module exists and produces public-safe proof pack
internal checker trace module is restricted and never appears in public response
golden hash storage exists and excludes volatile fields
root-only route guard runs and passes before integration PASS
final V5.3.1 integration report format is produced from actual command outputs
